requiredFiles.reason: Project known repository incidents to exactly id and owner while retaining an explicit missing-incident signal for the API layer.
Complete the existing public handler so it returns the required 200 response and maps missing incidents to the stable 404 response.
Add independently runnable unittest coverage for both the service projection and the public handler responses without a network listener.

acceptanceCriteria: GET /incident-owner/:incident_id returns status 200 for a known incident.
A known incident response body contains exactly id and owner.
A missing incident returns status 404 with the stable body {"error": "incident not found"}.
New Python regressions belong under tests/generated/test_*.py and use the standard-library unittest runner.
Handler verification exercises the public API entry point without opening a real network listener.
implementationChecklist: Update incident/service.py so build_incident_summary fetches through the existing repository dependency and returns exactly {"id": incident["id"], "owner": incident["owner"]} for a known incident.
Retain an explicit missing-incident outcome in incident/service.py, such as the existing KeyError when repository.get_incident returns None.
Update incident/api.py so the existing handler returns {"status": 200, "body": {"id": ..., "owner": ...}} for a successful service result.
Update incident/api.py to translate the service missing-incident outcome into exactly {"status": 404, "body": {"error": "incident not found"}}.
Do not modify incident/repository.py or existing verification-only suites.
Add tests/generated/test_incident_owner.py with mocked repository unit tests covering known-incident id/owner projection, exclusion of extra fields, and the missing-incident signal.
In the generated test module, call the API handler directly to verify exact 200 and 404 response dictionaries and confirm it invokes the backing service with the requested incident id.
verificationChecklist: Mock a repository incident containing id, owner, and extra fields; assert the service result is exactly the id/owner dictionary.
Mock a missing repository lookup and assert the service produces its defined missing-incident signal.
Directly invoke the public API handler with a successful service result and assert status 200 and an exact id/owner body.
Directly invoke the public API handler with the service missing-incident signal and assert status 404 and body exactly {"error": "incident not found"}.
Run only the generated regression module; it must not open a listener or make external network calls.
Unit test the backing behavior used by /incident-owner/:incident_id, including its empty-state response.
